metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_1094
metadata.comment:
Rationale: The paper describes that SGT-alpha binds to the synaptic vesicle protein CSP alpha. This is evidenced by robust yeast two hybrid data and recombinant protein binding studies. CSP-alpha is shown to simultaneously bind SGT-alpha and its other Hsc70 binding partner, thus forming a trimeric complex. In vitro studies show that the trimeric complex synergizes the ATPase and refolding activity of Hsc70. This is based on measurements of the ATPase activity of the trimeric complex and the refolding of a model substrate firefly luciferase. Comparing these activities imparted by individual or pairwise mixes of the components highlights the synergistic activity of the trimeric complex. The important evidence in terms of the synaptic localization is that the pool of the largely cytoplasmic SGT-alpha that is bound to synaptic vesicles is lost when these organelles are made from CSP alpha knockout mouse. These observations are critically dependent on a SGT-alpha antibody whose specificity is defined by a lack of immunoreactivity in pre-immune sera.. Experimental description: The papers clones the rat cDNA for the SGT-alpha and performs the protein interactions with Rat cDNA. There are several protein interaction and in vitro assays based on recombinant proteins expressed form Rat cDNA. However the best evidence for a direct synaptic localization directing function is from CSP-alpha knock out tissue.
Evidence from materials and methods: e.g. Full length DNAs for rat CSP1 and Hsc70; Yeast strain L40.......was sequentially transfected with bait..........and a rat brain cDNA library.
